title: aegilops peregrina var. intermedia
intro: Aegilops peregrina var. intermedia, also known as wild goatgrass or intermediate goatgrass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antifungal and antibacterial activities, due to the presence of certain compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The leaves and seeds of Aegilops peregrina var. intermedia can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the plant material in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
